title: Add customizable in-app notification triggers and appearance settings

requirements.text: Add persisted per-user notification preferences so each signed-in user can customize which in-app activities trigger notifications and how notifications visually appear.

quotes: customize which activities trigger notifications or how they visually appear?

acceptanceCriteria: Notification preferences are stored per Internet Identity principal and persist across refresh/login.
A reasonable default preference set exists for new users (e.g., some triggers enabled by default).
Preferences include at least (a) trigger toggles and (b) visual appearance options.
Frontend reads preferences on load and uses them to control notification behavior without requiring a redeploy.

requirements.text: Backend: extend the existing UserProfile model and APIs to support notification preferences, including query/update methods for the caller.

quotes: customize which activities trigger notifications or how they visually appear?

acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo UserProfile is extended to include notification preference fields (in addition to existing name).
Backend exposes functions to (a) get the caller's notification preferences and (b) update/save them for the caller.
Existing user profile data is not lost after upgrade (older stored profiles continue to load).
All logic remains in the single main actor file; a migration file is created only if required to preserve existing stable state.

requirements.text: Frontend: implement an in-app notification system (using the existing toast/toaster setup) that can emit notifications for supported activities and respects the user’s trigger preferences.

quotes: customize which activities trigger notifications or how they visually appear?

acceptanceCriteria: A new (non-placeholder) notifications hook/module exists to centralize notification logic (replacing the empty frontend/src/hooks/useNotifications.ts placeholder).
Notifications are shown only while the app is open (no push/background behavior).
At least two app activities can generate notifications and are individually toggleable (e.g., new chat messages; new/updated campfire or lake-location events; or other existing app activities).
When a trigger is disabled in preferences, the corresponding toast never appears.

requirements.text: Frontend: add a user-facing settings UI to customize notification triggers and visual appearance, and persist changes to the backend.

quotes: customize which activities trigger notifications or how they visually appear?

acceptanceCriteria: The app provides a clear place to configure notification settings (either within the existing Profile section or as a new navigation section).
The settings UI includes toggles for each supported notification trigger.
The settings UI includes controls for at least: toast position (top/bottom) and visual style (e.g., subtle vs. prominent and/or category colors/icons).
Saving settings shows success/error feedback using English user-facing text.
On reload, the UI reflects the saved settings fetched from the backend.

requirements.text: Frontend: apply the selected visual appearance settings consistently to all in-app notifications (styling, placement, and any icons/colors).

quotes: customize which activities trigger notifications or how they visually appear?

acceptanceCriteria: All notification toasts follow the chosen position and style settings.
Different notification categories (if implemented) use consistent visuals (e.g., the same color/icon mapping) across the app.
No direct edits are made to read-only component sources under frontend/src/components/ui; composition/wrapping is used instead.

constraints: Do not implement push notifications when the app is closed/minimized; notifications must remain in-app only.
Respect the single-actor Motoko backend architecture (all logic in backend/main.mo).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (compose them instead).
Do not modify immutable hook files listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., useActor/useInternetIdentity/main.tsx).
nonGoals: Integrating browser push notifications, service workers, or any background notification delivery.
Building a real-time websocket-based notification system.
